title: arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. crustacea
intro: Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. crustacea, also known as Bigberry manzanita, is a shrub species within the heather family (Ericaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate various health issues. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The leaves and berries of the Bigberry manzanita can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
